name: Google Flights Lookup
description: Search for flights from origin to destination via Google Search and extract the visible Flights widget text (best-effort).

browser_automation.description: Uses google.com/search?q=flights+from+ORIGIN+to+DESTINATION because the full Flights SPA at google.com/travel/flights is too brittle to extract reliably.

sequences.name: google_flights_search_extract
sequences.description: Navigate to a Google Search flights query and dump the result panel text.

required_variables.name: origin
required_variables.description: Departure city or airport code (e.g. NYC, JFK, New York).

required_variables.name: destination
required_variables.description: Arrival city or airport code (e.g. LON, LHR, London).

steps.name: Navigate to Google Search flights query

help.summary: Look up flights via Google Search and return the visible Flights widget text. Best-effort.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: Departure city or airport code. City names work; IATA codes (JFK, LHR) are most reliable.

examples.command: rzn-browser run google flights --param origin="NYC" --param destination="LON"
rzn-browser run google flights --param origin="SFO" --param destination="NRT"

help.returns: Object with one field, search_text — the inner text of the #search panel, including the Flights widget. Parsing into structured price/date/airline rows is left to the caller because Google rotates that DOM frequently.
notes: This workflow does not pick dates or filter cabins — it returns whatever the default widget shows.
For deeper flight queries (dates, cabin class), build on top of this output or open google/travel/flights directly via the orchestrator.
